text: Submits to John's baptism — suffer it to be so now, for thus it becometh us to fulfil all righteousness
Rebuffs Satan with Scripture — man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of God
Refuses to test God — it is written again, thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God
Commands Satan to depart — thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve
Cleanses the leper with a touch — I will, be thou clean — then charges him to show himself to the priest as a testimony
Responds to the centurion's request for his servant — I will come and heal him
Marvels at the centurion's faith — I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel
Heals the centurion's servant by his word — go thy way, and as thou hast believed, so be it done unto thee
Authorizes demons to enter the herd of swine with a single word — Go
Calls Matthew the tax collector from his booth — Follow me
Quiets the mourners at Jairus's house — give place, the maid is not dead, but sleepeth
Tests the blind men's faith — believe ye that I am able to do this?
Charges the healed blind men to silence — see that no man know it
Identifies John the Baptist as the prophesied Elijah — if ye will receive it, this is Elias, which was for to come
Likens this generation to children in the markets — they reject both John's fasting and the Son of Man's eating, but wisdom is justified of her children
Pronounces woe on Chorazin and Bethsaida — Tyre and Sidon would have repented in sackcloth and ashes had they seen such mighty works
Thanks the Father for hiding these things from the wise and revealing them unto babes — for so it seemed good in thy sight
Defends his disciples on the Sabbath — David ate the shewbread, priests profane the sabbath blameless, and one greater than the temple is here
Heals the man with the withered hand on the Sabbath — stretch forth thine hand
The tree is known by its fruit — out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh, and a good man brings forth good treasure
Rebukes the sign-seekers — an evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign, and none shall be given but the sign of the prophet Jonas
Nineveh and the Queen of the South will rise in judgment against this generation — for a greater than Jonas and Solomon is here
Redefines family — who is my mother and brethren? Behold my mother and my brethren — those who do God's will
Quotes Isaiah's prophecy fulfilled in dull-hearted hearers — they hear but understand not, see but perceive not, lest they be converted
Every scribe instructed in the kingdom is like a householder bringing forth from his treasure things new and old
Notes the rejection at Nazareth — a prophet is not without honour, save in his own country and in his own house
Tells the disciples to feed the multitude — they need not depart, give ye them to eat
Calls for the loaves and fishes before feeding the five thousand — bring them hither to me
Reassures the disciples on the storm-tossed sea — be of good cheer, it is I, be not afraid
Bids Peter walk on the water — Come
Summons the multitude to attend his teaching on defilement — hear, and understand
Every plant which my heavenly Father hath not planted shall be rooted up
Rebukes the disciples' dullness — are ye also yet without understanding that what enters the mouth goes into the belly?
Concludes the teaching on defilement — these are the things that defile a man, but eating with unwashen hands does not
Defines his earthly mission — I am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel
Tests the Canaanite woman — it is not meet to take the children's bread, and to cast it to dogs
Praises the Canaanite woman's faith — O woman, great is thy faith, be it unto thee even as thou wilt
Expresses compassion on the multitude — they have continued with me three days and have nothing to eat, I will not send them away fasting
Inquires before the feeding of the four thousand — how many loaves have ye?
Begins his rebuke about discerning signs — when it is evening, ye say, it will be fair weather, for the sky is red
Repeats the rebuke to sign-seekers — a wicked and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign, and none shall be given but the sign of Jonas
Rebukes the disciples for missing the point — O ye of little faith, do ye not remember the loaves of the five thousand and the four thousand?
Asks the disciples at Caesarea Philippi — whom do men say that I the Son of man am?
Presses the disciples for their own confession — but whom say ye that I am?
Blesses Peter for his confession — flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee, but my Father which is in heaven
Rebukes Peter for opposing the cross — get thee behind me, Satan, thou savourest not the things of God but the things of men
Reassures the disciples after the Transfiguration — arise, and be not afraid
Charges them to keep the vision secret — tell no man until the Son of man be risen again from the dead
Affirms Elijah has already come — they knew him not but did unto him whatsoever they listed; likewise shall the Son of man suffer
Laments the unbelief around him — O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I suffer you? Bring the boy hither to me
Teaches Peter about the temple tax — kings take tribute from strangers, not their own children, yet to avoid offense, take a coin from a fish's mouth
Declares his mission to seek the lost — for the Son of man is come to save that which was lost
Promises the twelve will sit on twelve thrones in the regeneration — when the Son of man sits in the throne of his glory, judging the twelve tribes of Israel
Reverses worldly ranks in the kingdom — but many that are first shall be last, and the last shall be first
Predicts his passion for the third time — the Son of man shall be betrayed, condemned, mocked, scourged, crucified, and the third day rise again
Stops for the two blind men outside Jericho — what will ye that I shall do unto you?
Sends two disciples for the colt before the triumphal entry — say the Lord hath need of them, and straightway he will send them
Cleanses the temple — my house shall be called the house of prayer, but ye have made it a den of thieves
Defends the children's praise — have ye never read, out of the mouth of babes and sucklings thou hast perfected praise?
Curses the fruitless fig tree — let no fruit grow on thee henceforward for ever
Counters the chief priests' challenge with a question — the baptism of John, whence was it, from heaven or of men?
Refuses to answer when they would not commit — neither tell I you by what authority I do these things
Silences the Pharisees with a question about Christ — whose son is he? If David in spirit calls him Lord, how is he his son?
Foretells the destruction of the temple — there shall not be left here one stone upon another that shall not be thrown down
Foretells his crucifixion at Passover — after two days is the feast, and the Son of man is betrayed to be crucified
Defends the woman who anointed him — she has done a good work, pouring ointment for my burial, and shall be remembered wherever the gospel is preached
Sends disciples to prepare the Passover — say the Master saith, my time is at hand, I will keep the passover at thy house with my disciples
Announces the betrayer at the Last Supper — verily I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me
Identifies Judas as the betrayer — he that dippeth his hand with me in the dish; woe to that man, it had been good for him not to have been born
Foretells the disciples' scattering — I will smite the shepherd and the sheep shall be scattered, but after I am risen I will go before you into Galilee
Foretells Peter's three denials — this night, before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice
Greets Judas at the betrayal — friend, wherefore art thou come?
Rebukes the use of the sword — all that take the sword shall perish; I could call legions of angels, but the scriptures must be fulfilled
Answers Pilate concerning kingship — Thou sayest
